brand.name: MRNote
hero.title: Write first. Save it when it matters.
hero.sub: MRNote is a notebook workspace for pages, sources, search, and graph work. Try writing first; sign up when you want to save and continue.
mode.title: Start with the first line, then bring sources and threads into the same place.
mode.capture.title: Capture
mode.capture.body: Start with a page. PDFs, screenshots, voice notes, and assistant replies can come in later.
mode.connect.title: Connect
mode.connect.body: The graph puts pages, sources, and conclusions next to each other so you can see where an idea came from.
mode.continue.title: Continue
mode.continue.body: Yesterday's title, follow-ups, and sources stay where you left them, ready to continue.
mode.review.title: Review
mode.review.body: Readings, review cards, and weekly notes stay available without digging through old chat logs.
digest.title.lineA: 30 seconds each morning to read yourself,
digest.lead: The daily digest closes yesterday's loose ends and picks three things worth doing today. The weekly reflection folds everything your notebook saw into a short story you'll want to re-read.
digest.fallback.guestHint: Sign in to see your own daily digest
exclusiveSection.statLineSuffix: {role}s already use MRNote as their daily {noun}
problem.title: Your work keeps spreading. Your context doesn't stay put.
problem.item1.title: Scattered notes
problem.item1.body: Meeting notes, reading highlights, PDFs, screenshots, and assistant replies land in different places. When you return, the important lines are missing.
problem.item2.title: Lost context
problem.item2.body: "Where did we decide that?" Fifteen minutes of search across pages, files, and chats later, you're still reconstructing it by hand.
problem.item3.title: Dropped follow-ups
problem.item3.body: Important follow-through slips: the note never became a page, the document never became study material, and the insight never made it into memory.
memorySection.title: A graph you can inspect, built from pages, files, and questions
memorySection.lead: MRNote does more than keep the latest few messages. Decisions in pages, quotes in sources, and questions from review cards stay as nodes with their sources attached. When you ask again, you can see why the answer came back and open the original text.
memorySection.graphAria: Memory graph illustration: a Memory V3 root surrounded by cross-surface memory nodes.
memorySection.stats.a.label: evidence nodes · 证据节点
memorySection.stats.b.label: concept ancestry levels · 层概念祖先
memorySection.stats.c.label: query p95 · 查询延迟
features.title: Write first, then add sources, search, and the graph.
feature1.title: Old pages do not go stale
feature1.body: Ask what a notebook already contains. MRNote looks through prior pages, sources, and graph nodes, then brings the source back with the answer.
feature1.bullets.0: Threads organized from notes, docs, and study material
feature1.bullets.1: Ask in plain language and open the original source
feature1.bullets.2: Private by default, never used to train models
feature2.title: More like a desk than a file tree
feature2.body: Pages, files, search, study, and the assistant can sit side by side. Read on the left, write on the right, and pull up search or the graph when needed.
feature2.bullets.1: Search across pages, files, study assets, memory, and AI actions
feature2.bullets.2: Return to the same notebook context instead of rebuilding it
feature3.title: A source does not end at upload
feature3.body: Upload a book or document, then create an overview page, ask questions, and make review cards. Useful conclusions can return to the notebook.
feature3.bullets.1: Q&A, summaries, and review cards tied to source passages
feature3.bullets.2: Useful conclusions continue into the memory graph
screenshot.title: A canvas, not a file tree.
screenshot.sub: Put pages, files, study material, and the assistant on the same canvas. Read, write, and ask side by side.
pricing.title: Simple pricing. Start free.
pricing.sub: Free forever on the basics. Upgrade when you need more.
plan.free.name: Free
plan.pro.name: Pro
plan.power.name: Power
plan.team.name: Team
cta.title: Start with one page.
cta.titleAccent: Save it to an account when it is worth keeping.
cta.sub: Free forever on the basics. Upgrade when you need more notebooks, study capacity, and graph features.
footer.tagline: A notebook workspace for people who write things through.
footer.lang.label: Language
meta.title: MRNote — A write-first notebook workspace
meta.description: A notebook workspace that keeps pages, sources, search, and the memory graph together.
og.title: MRNote — A write-first notebook workspace
og.description: Open a page and start writing. Save, organize sources, and use the graph when you are ready.
pricingPage.meta.title: Pricing
pricingPage.hero.title: Priced for notebook-native work.
pricingPage.hero.sub: Free forever on the basics. Upgrade when you need more notebooks, study capacity, AI actions, and memory depth. Cancel any time — your notebooks stay with you.
pricingPage.compare.title: Compare plans
pricingPage.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
pricingPage.faq.q1: Do I need a credit card to start?
pricingPage.faq.a1: No. Free is free forever — no card required. Pro comes with a 14-day free trial, and you'll only be charged if you decide to continue.
pricingPage.faq.q2: Can I pay with Alipay or WeChat Pay?
pricingPage.faq.a2: Yes, for one-time annual payments. Recurring subscriptions via Alipay and WeChat Pay are coming soon. Global cards are supported today.
pricingPage.faq.a3: You keep every notebook, every note, every memory. Your account moves to Free at the end of the current billing cycle — nothing is deleted.
pricingPage.faq.a4: Yes. Your notes, memories, and conversations are never used to train models — ours or anyone else's. Data is encrypted in transit and at rest.
pricingPage.faq.a5: Yes, any time. Export individual notebooks or your entire workspace as Markdown or JSON. Your data belongs to you.
pricingPage.faq.a6: Team is per-seat — the workspace owner pays for the whole team at $15 per seat per month. Members keep their own memory and notebooks, plus a shared workspace.
pricingPage.faq.q7: Do you offer a student discount?
pricingPage.faq.a7: Not today. If you're a student and cost is a barrier, email us at hello@mingrun-tech.com — we figure something out case by case.
pricingPage.faq.a8: Pro includes a 14-day free trial instead. That's a full two weeks to decide whether MRNote is worth the ten dollars before a charge happens.
